findings.summary: Fully-degenerate time-Bezier polygon [0,0,1,1] (interior handles collapsed onto the endpoints) at exactly t=t0 or t=t3: FCP's root-finder returns the OPPOSITE endpoint (u(0)=1.0, u(1)=0.0), while the engine returns the natural root. Every INTERIOR t and every strictly-increasing polygon matches to ~1e-9.
findings.not_a_bug: This input never occurs in real Motion: a stored time-Bezier's interior control points are keyframe HANDLE TIMES, kept strictly increasing (t0<t1<t2<t3). cases.bezierFind tests the faithful domain (strictly-increasing handles); we do NOT fit the engine to this FCP boundary artifact.
findings.revisit_if: A future .motr decode shows Motion CAN emit a collapsed/equal interior handle time.
